_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an aggregate index of key soft commodities including coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice, stands at 141 relative to its 2015 level of 100, indicating a roughly 41% increase over that baseline. This headline rise has been driven by recent price moves but has not been uniform across all components. The index captures global commodity prices and reflects market conditions over nearly a decade. Overall, the climb reflects significant but uneven price pressures within the basket.
notes.movers: The largest contributors to the basket's rise are coffee, which has nearly doubled (+99%) since 2015, and orange juice, up 34%, while wheat and sugar have increased more modestly by 19% and 13% respectively. These price movements are primarily due to weather-related events and crop disease rather than broad inflation trends. For example, citrus greening and hurricanes have impacted orange juice supplies, while frosts and droughts in arabica coffee-growing regions have constrained coffee output. Such developments illustrate the sensitivity of these thinly traded soft commodity markets to supply shocks.
notes.why: The data suggest that a small number of weather-exposed crops are responsible for most of the price gains in the breakfast basket. Since these commodities depend heavily on climatic conditions and can be affected over multiple seasons, input costs in these markets may remain elevated for years, underscoring persistent supply vulnerabilities.
